Patch Set 1:

In earlier versions of my original test-suite, I had code that would check the state of the test environment before sanity-lnet was executed, and then restore that environment when the test exited. Do we want to do something like that? You can see the code here https://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/35386/8/lustre/tests/sanity-dlc.sh

In brief:

  restore_mounts=false

  if is_mounted $MOUNT || is_mounted $MOUNT2; then
	  cleanupall || error "Failed cleanup prior to test execution"
	  restore_mounts=true
  fi

  restore_modules=false
  if module_loaded lnet ; then
	  cleanup_dlc || error "Failed to unload modules before test execution"
	  restore_modules=true
  fi

  cleanup_testsuite() {
	  rm -f $TMP/sanity-dlc*
	  cleanup_dlc
	  if $restore_mounts; then
		  setupall || error "Failed to setup Lustre after test execution"
	  elif $restore_modules; then
		  load_modules || error "Couldn't load modules after test execution"
	  fi
	  return 0
  }

I don't like the check_and_setup_lustre call because it'd be nice if you could run this test without waiting for lustre setup/teardown.

Patch Set 2:

I agree that, if we don't need to setup Lustre, then let's not set it up. I'm trying to understand why ldiskfs testing is suffering the problem described in LU-13002, but ZFS backed servers don't. 

So, probably checking the environment before testing is run and then restoring the environment is probably a good strategy for all of our test suites.

Patch Set 2:

One problem with setting up Lustre during the LNet testing is that this can cause errors for lustre due to high LNet traffic or network changes blocking Lustre connections. It would be better to not configure Lustre during these tests.

Patch Set 4:

Chris - Thank you for the patch. 

I've been playing with this issue for a little while today and, so far, the only way that I've found to allow test suites to run after sanity-lnet is to call cleanup_and_setup_lustre() at the end of sanity-lnet. I like your approach of just returning things to their previous state.

parents.subject: LU-12965 obdclass: remove assertion for imp_refcount

commit.subject: LU-13002 tests: change clean up in sanity-lnet
commit.message:
LU-13002 tests: change clean up in sanity-lnet

sanity-lnet should detect the state of the system before executing,
and restore that state when it has finished.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Test-Parameters: testlist=sanity-lnet,runtests
Signed-off-by: James Nunez <jnunez@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I3e3c5465789389e840efab516b35234cd61be901
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/36849
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>
Reviewed-by: Chris Horn <hornc@cray.com>
Reviewed-by: Shaun Tancheff <stancheff@cray.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
